headline.validated: Differentially validated against Trino, with DuckDB's official dsdgen as an independent data oracle.

scale.dominant: Decisive wins up to SF1 — across six of seven industry suites.
scale.progress: SF10+ (distributed) is closing fast: on a forced-distribution rig, TPC-DS SF1 went from 4.4x slower than Trino to 1.7x faster after the dynamic-filter pushdown fix. SSB is next — shipping build-side key sets (bloom filters) to workers.

leaps.title: The benchmark that lied
leaps.metric: Differential validation with DuckDB as an independent oracle: caught 16 vacuous TPC-DS queries and a Trino rounding bug (q75).

leaps.title: Our nemesis: TPC-DS q72
leaps.metric: Runtime-filter pushdown drops q72 from 10.7s to 0.77s.

leaps.title: Five layers of caching
leaps.metric: 8.8x faster than Trino across the suite.
